top_posts.text: I caught up with Matt Klein, one of the famed developers behind the Envoy Proxy, and had a chat about AI and why the hype machine has gone too far. I think the industry is putting machines over people and the tradeoff leans too far in the rent seeker's favor.

Hello #KubeCon day three! (Or two? Or four? Five?? Counting is hard)

The famous Kubernetes dress returns, too. Catch me floating around if you can!

We will be there this afternoon and Thursday afternoon. Hope to see you there!

Very impressed with the ventilation in the #kubecon public areas; the CO2 is usually in the 450-500 range; 435 in the press room with 10 people in here, which is about the same as being outside. Hope the session and lunch rooms and especially the parties are like that

So Kubecon EU is fucking ENORMOUS. I will report on numbers later but first impressions are just that it's simply very big. The @cncf.io is evidently still a critical foundation (pun intended) for modern software workloads.

I am having so much fun. Kubecon EU > Kubecon NA.

Specifically for my work, it’s bc most of the maintainers are based in the EU, so they can all come.
Just landed at SLC for Kubecon. Where’s everybody at?

I’m sad to be missing #KubeCon this week. It looks like it is going well and I wish I was there!

I am, however, heading up to Vancouver on Thursday for #AtmosphereConf. Looking forward to learning a ton and meeting new people. If you see me please say hi!

yeah this one's not great, tbh. litellm is pretty core infrastructure.

The news is out. I was selected as the next KubeCon co-chair 🙌 I will be helping with the content for #KubeConNA26 #KubeConEU27 and #KubeConNA27 🙂

Thank you @cncf.io team for the trust and honor 🙌 and many many folks 🙂

Looking forward to this new adventure and let's keep Cloud Native Moving 💪🤟
She go to KubeCon day two. No more talks from me, but that just means I have all day to wander around and say hi to everyone!

😭😭😭🙏 I got so many compliments about the talk. And the most important validation came from the most active maintainer on the project. 

Kubecon made ❤️

Based on the feedback I'm seeing, we should make KubeCon EU the place we start doing community awards and such. If it's the more heavily attended KubeCon, it's the flagship event, whether we want to admit it or not. The US isn't exactly encouraging folks to come here. #KubeCon

Attackers hijacked Axios, a popular JS HTTP client, publishing malicious versions that included a RAT. These were live for three hours, potentially affecting running production images on Kubernetes. The advisories focus on lockfiles, but users must also scan running container images and Dockerfiles.
recent_posts.embed_title:
axios got hijacked for 3 hours today - here's what the advisories aren't telling you about
  container images already running in production
